title: Josephus Riley
content:
آ آ آ آ The rum was rich and rare,
آ آ آ آ There were wagers in the air,
آ آ آ آ The atmosphere was rosy, and the tongues were wagging free;
آ آ آ آ But one was in the revel
آ آ آ آ Whose occiput was level
آ آ آ آ Plain Josephus Riley, from the North Countree.

آ آ آ آ The conversationï؟½s flow
آ آ آ آ Was not devoid of ï؟½blow,ï؟½
آ آ آ آ And neither was it wanting in the plain, colloquial ï؟½D.ï؟½
آ آ آ آ With a most ingenuous smile
آ آ آ آ ï؟½This here is not my style,ï؟½
آ آ آ آ Said plain Josephus Riley, from the North Countree.

آ آ آ آ ï؟½And I wouldnï؟½t be averse
آ آ آ آ To emptying my purse,
آ آ آ آ And laying some small wager with the present companee,
آ آ آ آ To cut the matter short
آ آ آ آ Foot racing is my forte,ï؟½
آ آ آ آ Said plain Josephus Riley, from the North Countree.

آ آ آ آ ï؟½I think itï؟½s on the cards
آ آ آ آ That I can run three hundred yards
آ آ آ آ (The match to be decided where you gentlemen agree)
آ آ آ آ Against your fleetest horse;
آ آ آ آ The race would prove a source
آ آ آ آ Of pleasure,ï؟½ said Josephus, from the North Countree.

آ آ آ آ ï؟½To equalise the task,
آ آ آ آ This little start I ask
آ آ آ آ The rider, ere he follows, must imbibe a cup of tea;
آ آ آ آ A simple breakfast-cup
آ آ آ آ He will have to swallow up.
آ آ آ آ Thatï؟½s me Josephus Riley, from the North Countree.ï؟½

آ آ آ آ Then a ï؟½knowing ï؟½unï؟½ looked wise,
آ آ آ آ ï؟½Begged to apologise;
آ آ آ آ But might he ask what tempï؟½rature the liquid was to be!
آ آ آ آ Would it come from out the pot
آ آ آ آ Milkless, steaming, boiling-hot?ï؟½
آ آ آ آ ï؟½Oh, not at all,ï؟½ said Riley, from the North Countree.

آ آ آ آ ï؟½Allow me to explain;
آ آ آ آ I do observe with pain,
آ آ آ آ This jocular reflection on my native honestee,
آ آ آ آ My bump of truth is huge,
آ آ آ آ Iï؟½d scorn a subterfugeï؟½
آ آ آ آ Said plain Josephus Riley, from the North Countree.

آ آ آ آ ï؟½Before the parties start
آ آ آ آ Iï؟½ll take the Judge apart
آ آ آ آ To prove, by tasting, whether I have tampered with
آ آ آ آ And I beg to state again
آ آ آ آ Your suspicions give me pain,ï؟½
آ آ آ آ Said plain Josephus Riley, from the North Countree.

آ آ آ آ Then they were all satisfied
آ آ آ آ That the match was ï؟½boneefied,ï؟½
آ آ آ آ The bond was signed, and Riley went to ï؟½preparateï؟½ the tea;
آ آ آ آ But his slow, ambiguous smile
آ آ آ آ Would have seemed to token guile
آ آ آ آ In any man but Riley, from the North Countree.

آ آ آ آ He brought the fatal cup
آ آ آ آ By its saucer covered up
آ آ آ آ The Judge examined its contents with awful gravitee,
آ آ آ آ Then read the papers oï؟½er,
آ آ آ آ But could not find a flaw:
آ آ آ آ ï؟½Wade in! Josephus Riley, from the North Countree.ï؟½

آ آ آ آ Then the ï؟½wagererï؟½ just bowed,
آ آ آ آ And, passing through the crowd,
آ آ آ آ He handed up the beverage unto the ï؟½wageree;ï؟½
آ آ آ آ And off across the flat,
آ آ آ آ Springing gaily, pit-a-pat,
آ آ آ آ Went plain Josephus Riley, from the North Countree.

آ آ آ آ But behind him what a yell
آ آ آ آ Of execration fell
آ آ آ آ From lips that lent themselves to shapes of great profanitee!
آ آ آ آ For the people of that town
آ آ آ آ Were done a lovely brown
آ آ آ آ By plain Josephus Riley, from the North Countree.

آ آ آ آ And hereï؟½s the reason why:
آ آ آ آ The tea was simply DRY,
آ آ آ آ You might eat it, but to drink it was impossibilitee;
آ آ آ آ But, curious to state,
آ آ آ آ Men did not appreciate
آ آ آ آ This humï؟½rous innovation from the North Countree.

آ آ آ آ Youï؟½ll understand, of course,
آ آ آ آ That wager was a source
آ آ آ آ Of very little profit to the hapless ï؟½wageree,ï؟½
آ آ آ آ And, dating from that day,
آ آ آ آ I much regret to say,
آ آ آ آ Men look askance at Riley, from the North Countree.
meaning:
This evocative piece by Barcroft Boake represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance, inviting the reader to reflect on the deeper themes of human experience and artistic expression.

In the broader context of Barcroft Boake's bibliography, these specific lines stand out for their clarity and poignancy. Whether you are searching for inspiration or a moment of quiet contemplation, this work offers a timeless perspective that transcends its original era.

At Linespedia, we have curated this specific poem to ensure it reaches a modern audience. We recommend sharing these lines as a digital poster or using them as a thoughtful caption to connect with others who appreciate the enduring beauty of verse.
